description:
You are acting as a monitoring-platform specialist who has just received a noisy application log and must prepare two artefacts that the on-call alerting pipeline will consume.

Current environment  
  • Your home directory is /home/user  
  • A source log already exists at /home/user/logs/combined.log (its exact contents are listed below).  
  • You do NOT need root rights; everything is done inside your own home.

Goal (5-10 shell commands are expected – feel free to combine pipes):  

1. Create a new directory called /home/user/alerts.  
   • Make sure it is writable by you (default user permissions are fine).  

2. Filter /home/user/logs/combined.log with a single regular-expression expression that simultaneously enforces BOTH of the following rules:  
     a) The line’s log-level token is either the literal word “ERROR” or “CRITICAL”.  
     b) The line contains an error-code token of the form “ERRnnnn” where nnnn ≥ 5000 (i.e. the first digit is 5-9).  
   • Lines matching rule (a) but whose error code is < 5000 MUST be excluded.  
   • Use a case-sensitive search; do NOT transform the file to lower/upper case.  

3. Write every line that survives the filter into /home/user/alerts/high_severity.log.  
   • Preserve the original order found in combined.log.  
   • Do not write duplicate lines even if the source contained duplicates.  
   • Terminate the file with a single trailing newline (important for automated checks).  

4. Next create /home/user/alerts/stats.log containing exactly ONE line whose format is:  
        Total high severity events: <N>  
   where <N> is the number of lines present in high_severity.log (just the integer, no extra spaces).  
   • Also end this file with a trailing newline.

5. When you are finished, you can cat both files to verify they look right, but the presence and exact contents of the two files will be used for grading.

Exact contents of the source file /home/user/logs/combined.log (11 lines, each ending with ‘\n’):

2023-03-21 10:15:43,221 INFO  [AUTH] User login succeeded uid=233  
2023-03-21 10:16:02,101 ERROR [API] ERR4152 - Failed to fetch resource id=34  
2023-03-21 10:16:45,312 WARNING [DB] Connection slow, t=527ms  
2023-03-21 10:17:11,998 ERROR [API] ERR5021 - Null pointer exception id=78  
2023-03-21 10:17:45,902 CRITICAL [SECURITY] ERR9001 - Multiple failed login attempts uid=122  
2023-03-21 10:18:10,455 INFO  [SCHED] Job 18 completed in 1234ms  
2023-03-21 10:19:03,211 ERROR [DB] ERR3004 - Timeout while querying orders  
2023-03-21 10:20:47,599 CRITICAL [API] ERR5500 - Data corruption detected id=12  
2023-03-21 10:21:12,772 DEBUG [CACHE] Cache miss key=session:233  
2023-03-21 10:21:45,001 ERROR [FS] ERR6400 - Disk full path=/var/data  
2023-03-21 10:23:11,650 ERROR [API] ERR5021 - Null pointer exception id=78  # duplicate of an earlier line for deduplication testing

What will be validated by the grading script:  

• The directory /home/user/alerts exists.  
• /home/user/alerts/high_severity.log exists and has exactly these four non-empty lines, in this order, and nothing else except a trailing newline:  
    1) 2023-03-21 10:17:11,998 ERROR [API] ERR5021 - Null pointer exception id=78  
    2) 2023-03-21 10:17:45,902 CRITICAL [SECURITY] ERR9001 - Multiple failed login attempts uid=122  
    3) 2023-03-21 10:20:47,599 CRITICAL [API] ERR5500 - Data corruption detected id=12  
    4) 2023-03-21 10:21:45,001 ERROR [FS] ERR6400 - Disk full path=/var/data  

• /home/user/alerts/stats.log exists, contains exactly:  
        Total high severity events: 4\n  

You can use any standard CLI utilities (grep, egrep, awk, sed, sort, uniq, etc.). Do NOT hard-code the four matching lines; generate them via your regex pipeline so the solution is reproducible if the source log changes.

A suitable regular expression for rule (b) is: ERR[5-9][0-9]{3}

Once both files match the specification, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial filesystem state added by the test harness:

1. Directory: /home/user/logs (permissions 755)  
2. File: /home/user/logs/combined.log (permissions 644) with the 11 lines listed in the public task description, each ending with '\n'.  
3. No /home/user/alerts directory yet.

Required end-state for successful grading:

1. /home/user/alerts must exist (permissions can be 755 or 700).  
2. /home/user/alerts/high_severity.log, permissions 644, contents EXACTLY:  
   2023-03-21 10:17:11,998 ERROR [API] ERR5021 - Null pointer exception id=78\n  
   2023-03-21 10:17:45,902 CRITICAL [SECURITY] ERR9001 - Multiple failed login attempts uid=122\n  
   2023-03-21 10:20:47,599 CRITICAL [API] ERR5500 - Data corruption detected id=12\n  
   2023-03-21 10:21:45,001 ERROR [FS] ERR6400 - Disk full path=/var/data\n  

3. /home/user/alerts/stats.log, permissions 644, contents EXACTLY:  
   Total high severity events: 4\n
name: task_000000_af2a348f
